'''Ribba''' was a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. Some [[Time/Legends|time]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Ribba owned a textile business which was, on its surface, legitimate. After the Hutt discovered that a [[Unidentified embezzler|member]] of the organization was embezzling funds, Ribba hired [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to track down the individual, with the condition that they be returned to Ribba alive. After tracking the embezzler to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]], the hunters [[Death/Legends|killed]] the wanted man during a [[Bar fight on Celanon|firefight]] within a [[Unidentified Celanon bar|bar]]. The preconditions of the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] dispelled, Ribba refused to pay the reward to the bounty hunters, who remained on Celanon to seek other work.<ref name="GTG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ribba was mentioned in [[1997]]'s [[No Disintegrations (West End Games)|''No Disintegrations'']], a sourcebook for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', in an adventure titled ''[[Gone To Ground]]'', written by [[Kevin M. MacGregor]]. Ribba's dealings with the hired bounty hunters was meant as backstory for the player characters of ''Gone to Ground'', who would embody the bounty hunters in the adventure. Ribba's gender is not mentioned in ''No Disintegrations''.<ref name="GTG" />
